pattern: There's no need to track a 2D grid at all -- a row/column pair updates independently per direction, and the final flattened index is just row*n+col at the end.
hints: Keep two running numbers, the current row and current column, both starting at zero -- you never need to build or index into an actual grid.
Each command only changes one of the two: UP/DOWN adjust the row, LEFT/RIGHT adjust the column.
After processing every command, convert the final row/column back into the flattened cell number the same way the grid numbers its cells.

edge_inputs.description: three moves on a 3x3 grid including a reversal
path returns exactly to the starting cell
hugs the top-left corner with moves that would go out of bounds if not careful, but never actually does
long straight run along a single row

stress.description: a random walk that always stays within the grid bounds, on a random grid size

complexity.time: O(m) where m is the number of commands
